title: fraxinus oxycarpa f. cuspidata
intro: Fraxinus oxycarpa f. cuspidata, also known as the narrow-leafed ash, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, derived from its bark and leaves. The narrow-leafed ash has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in detoxification. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in traditional medicine to create remedies for its numerous medicinal and health benefits.
